description_long: This bungalow features a striking, steeply pitched roof with pronounced upward-curving eaves at both ends, reminiscent of traditional Southeast Asian architecture. The roof is clad in dark brown wood, with a central ridge capped by a lighter, metallic-looking finial. The structure is elevated on short stilts, with a wraparound veranda supported by wooden posts, providing shaded outdoor space. The main building is rectangular, with a smaller, gabled extension projecting from one side, likely serving as an entrance or additional room. The exterior walls are primarily wooden, with some lighter panels visible on the side, and the overall design emphasizes open, airy spaces. The absence of visible windows on the main facades suggests a focus on privacy or traditional design elements.
description: A traditional wooden bungalow with a steep, upward-curving roof, wraparound veranda, and elevated on stilts, featuring a prominent gabled extension.
description_view_0: The view highlights the steep, central ridge of the roof and the symmetrical upward curves at both ends, with the veranda and supporting posts visible below.
description_view_1: This side view showcases the full length of the roof's upward-curving eaves and the gabled extension projecting from the right, as well as the open veranda beneath.
description_view_2: The opposite end view reveals the gabled extension more clearly, along with the lighter wall panels and the stilted base of the bungalow.
description_view_3: This side view mirrors view 1 but without the gabled extension, emphasizing the uninterrupted expanse of the roof and the veranda below.
attribute_window_arrangement: minimal visible windows, likely grouped on sides or rear
